explanation:
**Personal finance and investing** use universal mathematical principles (compound interest, time value of money, risk-return optimization). This calculator applies industry-standard formulas used in financial markets worldwide.

## How It's Calculated

Profit and loss on perpetual futures positions.

This calculator applies the formula with your input values, checking typical ranges and alerting you to unreasonable values.

## Typical Benchmarks by Timeframe

| Timeframe | S&P 500 | 10y Bonds | Cash |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 year | Highly variable | 4-5% | 3-5% |
| 5 years | ~8% avg | ~4% | Inflation |
| 10 years | ~9-10% | ~4% | <Inflation |
| 20+ years | ~10% nominal | ~5% | N/A |

## Investment Principles

- **Stick to the plan** during crises. Emotions are your worst advisor.
- **Diversify**: don't concentrate in a single asset, sector, or country.
- **Keep costs low**: expense ratios >1% annually eat 30% of your returns over 30 years.
- **Long horizon**: equities work over 10+ years, not 1 year.
- **Rebalance**: once per year, no more.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference tool only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult with a specialist. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: What is P&L in futures trading?
faq.a: P&L (Profit & Loss) is your gain or loss on a futures position, calculated as (closing price - entry price) × position size.
Perpetual futures are derivative contracts that track an underlying asset (like Bitcoin) with leverage, allowing you to go long or short. They have no expiration date.
Leverage lets you control a larger position with less capital. 10x leverage means you control 10x your margin. Higher leverage = higher risk and reward.

faq.q: What's the difference between long and short?
faq.a: Long: you profit if the price goes up. Short: you profit if the price goes down.
ROE (Return on Equity) is your profit/loss percentage relative to your margin, not the notional position value.
Funding rates are paid every 8 hours between long and short traders to keep perpetual futures prices aligned with spot prices. They can be positive or negative.
Unrealized P&L is your current gain/loss while the position is open. Realized P&L is locked in only after you close the position.

faq.q: What trading fees do I pay?
faq.a: Maker fees (limit orders) are cheaper than taker fees (market orders). Typical rates are 0.02%-0.1% per trade depending on the exchange.

faq.q: What returns should I expect from crypto markets?
faq.a: S&P 500 historical average: ~10% nominal annually (7% adjusted for inflation). Crypto is far more volatile—past performance doesn't predict future results.
